headline: How The Evolving Story For First Horizon (FHN) Reflects Mixed Analyst Targets And Valuation Shifts
summary: First Horizon is back in focus as analysts adjust their price targets, with one firm lifting its view to US$28.50 while others trim theirs by roughly US$1 to US$2. These shifts reflect a reassessment of valuation, earnings power and interest rate sensitivity. Some research highlights solid profitability potential, while others flag concerns about execution, growth and funding costs. Read on to see how to interpret these moves and what to watch as the analyst narrative around First Horizon...

headline: How Hancock Whitney (HWC) Is Reframing Its Story Around Profitability Execution And Valuation
summary: Hancock Whitney’s updated analyst model now points to a Fair Value price target of US$77.67 per share, compared with the prior US$76.44. This marks a modest shift in how equity value is being framed. That move lines up with recent analyst commentary that balances optimism around profitability with caution on execution risk and the range of price targets clustering around levels such as US$81. Read on to see what is driving this evolving narrative and how you can keep track of the key signals...
